item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it but would really like to know how it responds to video records. Does it react to a camera the same as a person? If it doesn't then what does it see that people cant see?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You didn't wash behind your ears or nothin'.
Frighteningly hilarious anomaly if you reflect on the basic anomalous traits this shower curtain possesses.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It says at the bottom that testing is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You would think there would be more to the test logs after they found someone who comes out unharmed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cleanse yourself of your sins, else become them, it would seem.

What a cleanly delivered idea - not too overt, yet a large enough trail of crumbs to pick up all the important bits of what's going on.

item.title: Test 1602-11
item.content:encoded: So what is the last test all about? I'm missing something here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changing my vote because, while not bad persay, this feels like an amalgamation of every clichéd horror trope there is. The imagery is somewhat disturbing but the whole thing simply didn't interest me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn this was scary. I always check behind the curtain when I go to the bathroom and now you've given me an excuse. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet Aperture Science made this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this thing tries to cure bad people with immersion therapy and then punishes them with the form if they dont change doesnt it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concept seemed a little bit bland, but the test logs really brought it to life nicely. Squicky and creepy, and the final test left me with a real sense of 'what's really going on here?' - left me wanting more, in the best possible way. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this skip. Enjoyin' your articles. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm having some trouble fitting in how a Caucasian male, aged 58 and a high-ranking employee at a Fortune 500 company, would end up being class D personnel. I can picture what basically amounts to a senior Patrick Bateman, but then I have a hard time picturing him expressing significant frustration over his estrangement with his children and ex-wife.
I'm sure a plausible backstory exists or can be imagined, but it just struck me as odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch! I feeks eet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a mistake under the "procedure" section of Test 1602-18. They gave the gun to the D-class, not the shower curtain, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tag guide defines artifacts as SCPs that "[are or were] originally created by humans." I think this fits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since this was my first SCP, I thought I'd come back to it now that I've done a few more. There were a few things I wasn't happy with, so I altered the containment procedures, cleaned up the description, and added a recovery story. I also included an extra test log in which the subject survives their abduction unharmed, which I think strengthens the underlying theme I had been aiming for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i seem to have missed the point at which this became an artifact. but upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your worst nightmare is behind the curtain… and you're it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How many fetuses do you think it would take to drag a full grown man across a room, against his will?
I think several hundred would be a reasonable minimum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it a lot, yet another SCP that makes me scared of my shower.
Just one nitpick, that I actually have with lots of SCPs, I think your percentages are a bit too specific. "88% of test subjects" just makes it seem like they tested it a ridiculous amount of times. Also "22% of instances" once again suggests there were a hell of a load of the babies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It quickly becomes clear what SCP-1602-A is, but you never actually give a solid description/definiton of it in the normal Foundation style.

Overall, though, me gusta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I remember reading the draft for this. I still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is missing some things I would expect to see, such a recovery log and possibly a camera/video test. However, I think the writing is pretty good and the take on the idea interesting enough to be upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe "generates an instance of SCP-1602-A" is a better way of cleaning up that sentence in particular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as the description goes, it was a bland article. However, the testing log was interesting. I am with Scantron, and Mr. Wilt on this one. The tone and description need work. For now I will remain neutral in my voting on this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of other scips that play on a victims fears or regrets, and I feel like this one only stands out because there's so much grimdark crammed into the experiment log. I also groaned a bit as soon as I saw a shower curtain with a shadowy hand behind it. That's a -1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit that I wasn't expecting much after the Description, but for some reason the testing logs and their descriptions of a person's insecurities dragging them to another place and turning them into said insecurities really did it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spell-checking. Errgh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, overall I like it. I don't see anything really wrong with it, and I don't see any problems left from the draft (not that there were very many to begin with).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredibly verbose. Lots of instances where words could be trimmed to improve flow.

SCP-1602 in its active state, generating an instance of SCP-1602-A in the presence of D-1602-3. The image comes from video footage of Experiment 1602-3.

Could be:

SCP-1602 in its active state, generating an instance of SCP-1602-A.

Or:

SCP-1602 during Experiment 1602-3.

Also, I'd like to get a better description of 1602-A in the actual description. I thought they were actual shadows instead of physical entities before I got to the log. That kind of reveal can work (fridge logic, et al), but this SCP seems too straightforward for that. It needs a straightforward description, then the creepy log to give it the dramatic element.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like there's nothing here that I haven't seen done better in other SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not bad, but I'm just not digging it. I feel it relies too heavily on the experiment log to make it interesting, and while the experiments do add a bit of intrigue, their results seem a little… predictable. Your containment procedures also seem rather lax, with nothing more than a combination lock to keep somebody from stealing a potentially dangerous item, and your researcher insert is kind of rubbing me the wrong way.

Your writing could also use a bit of work, with oddities like:

…as SCP-1602 appears to generate an extradimension space behind it when active

…an instance of SCP-1602-A will be generated, its silhouette visible behind the curtain.

but overall your tone is fairly decent.

I do think this is pretty good for a first article, and with a bit of work I think I'd enjoy this quite a bit, but as it is I'm not feeling it enough to give it more than a neutral vote.

item.title: Whee!
item.content:encoded:
Here it is, folks: my first article! Hope you like it (or at least find it relatively adequate)!

I'm still on the fence about the interview log. If it's not working, let me know and I'll cut it.

Changed photo in 2019 to comply with changes in licensing standards: Shadow Play by chispita_666.

Discover more of my weird ramblings in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
